@model.description: Declares the strongly-typed model class for a Razor view, enabling IntelliSense and compile-time checking for model property access throughout the view template with @Model references.

@inject.description: Injects a registered dependency injection service into the Razor view as a property, making application services like configuration, localization, and custom services available in templates.

@page.description: Marks a Razor file as a Razor Page with an optional custom route template, making it directly routable without a controller. The page handles HTTP requests via OnGet and OnPost handler methods.

Html.Partial.description: Renders a partial view synchronously and returns the HTML content. For better performance, use Html.PartialAsync or the partial tag helper to avoid blocking the request thread.

Html.Raw.description: Returns unencoded HTML markup that is not further HTML-encoded by Razor output. Use with trusted content only as it bypasses the default XSS protection provided by automatic HTML encoding.

Html.ActionLink.signature: Html.ActionLink(linkText, actionName, controllerName, routeValues, htmlAttributes) -> IHtmlContent
Html.ActionLink.description: Generates an HTML anchor element with an href attribute pointing to the specified controller action, automatically resolving routes and encoding parameters for MVC navigation links.

ViewBag.description: A dynamic property bag for passing data from controllers to views without strong typing. Uses C# dynamic dispatch so properties are resolved at runtime with no compile-time type checking.

ViewData.description: A dictionary-based mechanism for passing data from controllers to views using string keys. Values are typed as object and require casting. ViewBag is a dynamic wrapper around ViewData.

Title.description: Conventionally used to set the page title displayed in the layout _Layout.cshtml.

TempData.description: A short-lived dictionary for passing data between two consecutive requests, typically used for redirect-based status messages. Data persists through one subsequent request then is automatically deleted.

Url.Action.description: Generates a URL string for the specified controller action using the application routing configuration. Returns a properly encoded URL without creating a full anchor element.

Url.Content.description: Converts a virtual application-relative path starting with tilde to an absolute path, resolving the application root for referencing static files like CSS, JavaScript, and images.

@section.description: Defines a named content section in a Razor view that can be rendered at a specific location in the layout page using RenderSection. Enables placing scripts, styles, or content in layout-defined areas.

@using.description: Adds a C# using directive to the Razor view, making types from the specified namespace available without fully qualifying their names throughout the template file.

@code.description: Defines a code block in Blazor components containing C# member declarations including fields, properties, methods, and event handlers that form the component logic and state.

RenderSection.description: Renders the content of a named section defined in a child view within the layout page. The required parameter controls whether views must define the section or can omit it.

RenderBody.description: Renders the main content of a child view within the layout page at the location where RenderBody is called. Every layout page must include exactly one call to RenderBody.
